name: Dallas Goedert
headline: Career-high scores mask touchdown regression coming for Goedert
body:
Goedert's 2025 looked like a breakout, but the engine tells a quieter story. He turned 82 targets into 60 grabs and 591 yards while finishing TE14 with 125.1 points, and his efficiency stayed elite: a 0.441 EPA per play and a 58.5% success rate that few tight ends touch. The catch is the touchdowns. Eleven scores, many on shovel passes inside the five, drove that fantasy line despite a pedestrian 1.44 yards per route run and a 17.7% target share that screams secondary option.

That scoring spike is the regression flag. He cleared 15 red-zone looks and nine end-zone targets, but more than double his previous single-season high in touchdowns rarely sticks, especially with a new play-caller and no guarantee the goal-line role survives. Back for a ninth year as Philadelphia's starter, he profiles as a steady late-round TE12-type bet — useful, but priced for the volume, not the touchdown luck.
